evidence.scientific_clarity: Sankey flow from Raw→Cleaned→Filtered→Train/Val/Test is visible and proportional; 'Reject' node appears but the 120-rejected flow from ingestion is not clearly distinguishable as a separate rejection path, slightly obscuring the brief's claim.
evidence.typography: PDF uses DejaVu Sans Type3 bitmapped fonts (confirmed via 'strings figure.pdf'); no pdf.fonttype=42 set in gen_figure.py; plotly fallback used Arial but matplotlib fallback (which actually ran) defaults to DejaVu Sans Type3.
evidence.palette: Source-consistent flow coloring is implemented; Reject node uses red (#d62728) which is appropriate for a directional/negative meaning; palette is mostly CB-safe (blue/green/orange/purple) but red+green co-presence could trouble deuteranopes.
evidence.layout_density: 10×5.5 inch figure with nodes and flows; layout is reasonable but y-axis range is ±750 with actual content spanning roughly ±600, leaving modest dead space top/bottom; no label overlap observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: No legend (colors explained by node labels inline); caption matches figure content; title 'Dataset Preparation Flow' duplicates caption; a reviewer might ask for explicit rejection-path annotation and CB accessibility statement.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is a real PDF (not rasterized image-in-PDF), but fonts are embedded as Type3 (bitmapped glyphs) rather than TrueType/Type42; text is not properly selectable/scalable.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (16 KB), figure.png (58 KB), figure.svg (28 KB), caption.tex (471 B), and gen_figure.py (5 KB) all exist and are non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption provides specific numerical claims (1,120 raw → 1,000 cleaned, 120 rejected; 60 removed during cleaning; 820 filtered; 600/120/100 split); explains flow-width encoding and color semantics; cites generation tool and data source.

overall_strengths: Caption is detailed and numerically specific, matching the figure's data precisely and explaining visual encoding conventions.
All required output files (PDF, PNG, SVG, caption.tex, gen_figure.py) are present and non-trivial; source-consistent flow coloring clearly shows which stage each flow originates from.
overall_weaknesses: PDF fonts are DejaVu Sans Type3 bitmaps (matplotlib fallback executed instead of plotly); pdf.fonttype=42 was never set, making text non-selectable and uneditable in vector editors.
Red (#d62728) used for 'Reject' node co-exists with green ('Cleaned'), creating a red-green pair that is inaccessible to deuteranopes; no colorblind-safe alternative was applied.
